"vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:L/VA:N/SC:L/SI:L/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-1321", "name": "Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')"}], "description": "## Impact
A prototype pollution vulnerability allowed a crafted public webhook payload to inject attacker-controlled fields into workflow data during internal object copying. These fields could be surfaced and consumed as normal values by downstream built-in nodes.

Where a workflow combines a public webhook with action nodes that consume the resulting fields, an attacker could cause the workflow to act as a confused deputy — targeting unintended records or issuing outbound requests using the workflow owner's configured credentials.

## Patches
The issue has been fixed in n8n versions 2.25.7, and 2.26.2. Users should upgrade to one of these versions or later to remediate the vulnerability.

## Workarounds
If upgrading is not immediately possible, administrators should consider the following temporary mitigations:
- Avoid exposing public (unauthenticated) webhook workflows that pass incoming data through transform nodes into action nodes with sensitive credentials or database operations.
- Limit workflow creation and editing permissions to fully trusted users only.

These workarounds do not fully remediate the risk and should only be used as short-term mitigation measures.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00259, "percentile": 0.17131}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-16T19:00:41Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-54306"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-23T17:17:06Z", "published_at": "2026-06-16T19:00:41Z", "references": ["https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/security/advisories/GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-54306", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/n8n-io/n8n/security-advisories/GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n", "summary": "n8n: Prototype Pollution enables confused-deputy execution via public webhooks", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-20T13:45:15Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-2vff-hj5x-8gq7", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.26.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "n8n"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2.26.0, < 2.26.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "2.25.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "n8n"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.25.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: n8n: Prototype Pollution enables confused-deputy execution via public webhooks
